info.title: STEP Remote System Administration
info.description:
Interface for remote system management for clients without server file system access.
The 'System Administration' user privilege is required for all resource operations.

post.description: Endpoint for restarting the STEP system. Notice that as the Remote System Administration API and the Swagger UI is served by the STEP system, both will be unavailable until the system has been started again.

200.description: Server is about to be restarted

get.description: Endpoint for getting information about which extensions are configured to be installed on the STEP system. The 'source' query parameter determines whether the returned information shows the currently effective list of extensions (value 'current', default) or the list that will be in effect after the next restart (value 'desired').

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Source of the extensions metadata. Can be either "current" (to list currently applied extensions metadata) or "desired" (to list extensions metadata that will be applied on next restart)

put.summary: Sets the desired list of extensions
put.description:
Endpoint for specifying which extensions should be deployed on the STEP system as part of the next system restart. Body must be an array of strings, where each supplied string is a valid recipe for the extension on the Stibo Systems "updates" server. Notice that the list is overwritten with each request. I.e. the full list of desired extensions must be provided with each request. Use GET /step/extensions to get the current list.
Will return 403 if the extension developer is not trusted on the STEP system. Use the information provided in the 403 response with the PUT /step/extensions/partner/trust/{certificate-id} endpoint to establish this trust.

200.description: The recipes were correctly saved and will be applied after a server restart

403.description: Not all partner's certificates are trusted. It's forbidden to put such recipes on system until the partner certificates are marked as trusted

put.summary: Establishes trust for a specific developer party
put.description: Endpoint for trusting a specific developer party. The certificate ID to be passed in the URL will be returned in 403 responses for the PUT /step/extensions endpoint in the case where the developer behind an extension is not already trusted on the STEP system.

parameters.name: certificate-id
parameters.description: Id of the partner certificate to trust

200.description: The trust to partner certificate has been correctly established

400.description: Invalid partner certificate has been provided

ClusterRestartResponse.description: Response for STEP system restart operation

recipe.description: Recipe url of the extension component

ExtensionPartnerCertificate.description: Response for putting extensions metadata operation

trusted.description: Boolean indicating whether current partner certificate is trusted by system

componentRecipe.description: Recipe url of the component signed by partner certificate

ExtensionPartnerTrustResponse.description: Response for putting extension partner certificate trust

PutExtensionsResponse.description: Response for putting extensions metadata operation

PutExtensionsTrustRequiredResponse.description: Error response for putting extensions metadata operation, when obtaining trust to selected partner is required

message.description: Extended error information. Notice that error messages may change without warning and that programming logic should not rely upon the content of the message.

httpReason.description: The name of the HTTP status code
